description_long: This object is a vintage wall-mounted corded telephone, featuring a rectangular wooden body with a polished finish. The front panel displays two metallic bells at the top, a large circular mouthpiece, and a rotary dial with visible finger holes and numbers. A black cylindrical handset is attached to the left side of the body by a straight cord, and the handset rests in a metal cradle. The side views reveal the depth of the wooden box and the protruding mouthpiece, while the back is plain wood. The metallic components, including the bells and rotary dial, have a brass-like appearance, adding to the antique aesthetic. This type of phone is reminiscent of early 20th-century telecommunication devices commonly found in homes or small offices.
description: A vintage wall-mounted corded telephone with a wooden body, brass bells, rotary dial, and a black handset attached by a cord.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden body, brass bells, rotary dial, mouthpiece, and the black handset hanging on the left.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the wooden box, the protruding mouthpiece, and the side profile of the handset.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain wooden back panel and the cord extending from the handset.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the handset, the metal cradle, and the side profile of the rotary dial and bells.
